comment_text.sn44.2:16.2: This passage relies on the label usage of the nominative, as indicated by the particle -ti. It is a tricky passage to render consistently and coherently, so I make the syntax explicit.
comment_text.sn44.2:17.2: See note on SN 22.85:10.2 for a discussion of this passage.

root_text.sn44.2:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn44.2:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā anurādho bhagavato avidūre araññakuṭikāyaṁ viharati.
root_text.sn44.2:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā aññatitthiyā paribbājakā yenāyasmā anurādho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā anurādhena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn44.2:1.4: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā āyasmantaṁ anurādhaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:1.5: “yo so, āvuso anurādha, tathāgato uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto, taṁ tathāgato imesu catūsu ṭhānesu paññāpayamāno paññāpeti:
root_text.sn44.2:1.6: ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:1.7: “Yo so, āvuso, tathāgato uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto, taṁ tathāgato aññatra imehi catūhi ṭhānehi paññāpayamāno paññāpeti:
root_text.sn44.2:1.8: ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā”ti.
root_text.sn44.2:1.9: Evaṁ vutte, te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā āyasmantaṁ anurādhaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:1.10: “so cāyaṁ bhikkhu navo bhavissati acirapabbajito, thero vā pana bālo abyatto”ti.
root_text.sn44.2:1.11: Atha kho te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā āyasmantaṁ anurādhaṁ navavādena ca bālavādena ca apasādetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.sn44.2:2.1: Atha kho āyasmato anurādhassa acirapakkantesu aññatitthiyesu paribbājakesu etadahosi:
root_text.sn44.2:2.2: “sace kho maṁ te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā uttariṁ puccheyyuṁ,
root_text.sn44.2:2.3: kathaṁ byākaramāno nu khvāhaṁ tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ vuttavādī ceva bhagavato assaṁ, na ca bhagavantaṁ abhūtena abbhācikkheyyaṁ, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākareyyaṁ, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgaccheyyā”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:2.4: Atha kho āyasmā anurādho yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā anurādho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.2:2.6: Atha kho, bhante, sambahulā aññatitthiyā paribbājakā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā mayā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn44.2:2.7: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho, bhante, te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā maṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:2.8: ‘yo so, āvuso anurādha, tathāgato uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto, taṁ tathāgato imesu catūsu ṭhānesu paññāpayamāno paññāpeti:
root_text.sn44.2:2.10: “neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti vā’ti?
root_text.sn44.2:2.11: Evaṁ vuttāhaṁ, bhante, te aññatitthiye paribbājake etadavocaṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:2.12: ‘yo so, āvuso, tathāgato uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto, taṁ tathāgato aññatra imehi catūhi ṭhānehi paññāpayamāno paññāpeti:
root_text.sn44.2:2.14: “neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti vā’ti.
root_text.sn44.2:2.15: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā maṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:2.16: ‘so cāyaṁ bhikkhu navo bhavissati acirapabbajito thero vā pana bālo abyatto’ti.
root_text.sn44.2:2.17: Atha kho maṁ, bhante, te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā navavādena ca bālavādena ca apasādetvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.sn44.2:2.18: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhante, acirapakkantesu tesu aññatitthiyesu paribbājakesu etadahosi:
root_text.sn44.2:2.19: ‘sace kho maṁ te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā uttariṁ puccheyyuṁ, kathaṁ byākaramāno nu khvāhaṁ tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ vuttavādī ceva bhagavato assaṁ, na ca bhagavantaṁ abhūtena abbhācikkheyyaṁ, dhammassa cānudhammaṁ byākareyyaṁ, na ca koci sahadhammiko vādānuvādo gārayhaṁ ṭhānaṁ āgaccheyyā’”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:5.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:7.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:11.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:13.1: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, kallaṁ nu taṁ samanupassituṁ:
root_text.sn44.2:15.1: “Tasmātiha, anurādha, yaṁ kiñci rūpaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ rūpaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn44.2:15.5: yaṁ kiñci viññāṇaṁ atītānāgatapaccuppannaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā oḷārikaṁ vā sukhumaṁ vā hīnaṁ vā paṇītaṁ vā yaṁ dūre santike vā, sabbaṁ viññāṇaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti evametaṁ yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya daṭṭhabbaṁ.
root_text.sn44.2:15.6: Evaṁ passaṁ, anurādha, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpasmimpi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn44.2:15.7: Nibbindaṁ virajjati; virāgā vimuccati; vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn44.2:15.8: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.sn44.2:17.2: rūpaṁ, vedanaṁ, saññaṁ, saṅkhāre, viññāṇaṁ tathāgatoti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:17.5: ayaṁ so arūpī avedano asaññī asaṅkhāro aviññāṇo tathāgatoti samanupassasī”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:17.7: “Ettha ca te, anurādha, diṭṭheva dhamme saccato thetato tathāgate anupalabbhiyamāne kallaṁ nu te taṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ—
root_text.sn44.2:17.8: yo so, āvuso, tathāgato uttamapuriso paramapuriso paramapattipatto, taṁ tathāgato aññatra imehi catūhi ṭhānehi paññāpayamāno paññāpeti:
root_text.sn44.2:17.10: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā”ti?
root_text.sn44.2:17.13: Pubbe cāhaṁ, anurādha, etarahi ca dukkhañceva paññāpemi dukkhassa ca nirodhan”ti.

translation_text.sn44.2:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn44.2:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Anurādha was staying not far from the Buddha in a wilderness hut.
translation_text.sn44.2:1.3: Then several wanderers of other religions went up to Venerable Anurādha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.2:1.4: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side and said to him:
translation_text.sn44.2:1.5: “Reverend Anurādha, when a realized one is describing a realized one—a supreme person, highest of people, who has reached the highest point—they describe them in these four ways:
translation_text.sn44.2:1.6: After death, a realized one still exists, or no longer exists, or both still exists and no longer exists, or neither still exists nor no longer exists.”
translation_text.sn44.2:1.7: “Reverends, when a realized one is describing a realized one—a supreme person, highest of people, who has reached the highest point—they describe them other than these four ways:
translation_text.sn44.2:1.8: After death, a realized one still exists, or no longer exists, or both still exists and no longer exists, or neither still exists nor no longer exists.”
translation_text.sn44.2:1.9: When he said this, the wanderers said to him,
translation_text.sn44.2:1.10: “This mendicant must be junior, recently gone forth, or else a foolish, incompetent senior mendicant.”
translation_text.sn44.2:1.11: Then, after rebuking Venerable Anurādha by calling him “junior” and “foolish”, the wanderers got up from their seat and left.
translation_text.sn44.2:2.1: Soon after they had left, Anurādha thought,
translation_text.sn44.2:2.2: “If those wanderers were to inquire further,
translation_text.sn44.2:2.3: how should I answer them so as to repeat what the Buddha has said, and not misrepresent him with an untruth? How should I explain in line with his teaching, so that there would be no legitimate grounds for rebuttal and criticism?”
translation_text.sn44.2:2.4: Then Venerable Anurādha went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn44.2:5.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn44.2:7.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn44.2:7.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn44.2:11.1: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn44.2:13.1: “But if it’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, is it fit to be regarded thus:
translation_text.sn44.2:13.2: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self’?”
translation_text.sn44.2:15.1: “So, Anurādha, you should truly see any kind of form at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all form—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn44.2:15.5: consciousness at all—past, future, or present; internal or external; solid or subtle; inferior or superior; far or near: all consciousness—with right understanding: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn44.2:15.6: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn44.2:15.7: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn44.2:15.8: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.sn44.2:16.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to form?”
translation_text.sn44.2:16.4: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to feeling?” …
translation_text.sn44.2:16.13: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies in relation to form?”
translation_text.sn44.2:16.15: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies apart from form?”
translation_text.sn44.2:16.17: “Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies in relation to feeling?”
translation_text.sn44.2:17.2: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to one who has form?” … “… feeling?” … “… perception?” … “… choices?” … “… consciousness?”
translation_text.sn44.2:17.5: Do you consider that the label ‘realized one’ applies to that one who is without form?” … “… feeling?” … “… perception?” … “… choices?” … “… consciousness?”
translation_text.sn44.2:17.7: “In that case, Anurādha, since a realized one is not found by you as a genuine fact in this very life, is it cogent for you to declare:
translation_text.sn44.2:17.8: ‘Reverends, when a realized one is describing a realized one—a supreme person, highest of people, who has reached the highest point—they describe them other than these four ways:
translation_text.sn44.2:17.9: After death, a realized one still exists, or no longer exists, or both still exists and no longer exists, or neither still exists nor no longer exists’?”
translation_text.sn44.2:17.13: In the past, as today, what I describe is suffering and the cessation of suffering.”

variant_text.sn44.2:17.7: saccato thetato tathāgate anupalabbhiyamāne → saccato vā tathato vā anupabbhanīyasabhāvo (cck, sya1ed); saccato vā tathato vā anupalabbhiyamāno (sya2ed, mr); saccato thetato tathāgate anupalabbhyamāne (pts1ed); saccato thetato tathāgate anupalabbhamāne (?) | veyyākaraṇaṁ → vyākaraṇāya (bj)
